title: India Successfully Tests Indigenous NASM-SR Naval Anti-Ship Missile Salvo
summary: India conducted the first salvo launch of its indigenous short-range naval anti-ship missile from a helicopter, achieving all objectives.
contents:
# India Successfully Tests Indigenous NASM-SR Naval Anti-Ship Missile Salvo

## Overview
India conducted the first salvo launch of its indigenous short-range naval anti-ship missile from a helicopter, achieving all objectives.

## Details
The Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) and Indian Navy fired two NASM-SR missiles in quick succession from a helicopter platform over the Bay of Bengal. Both missiles struck their sea targets at the waterline with pinpoint precision, demonstrating rapid-fire launch and advanced targeting. The missile features a 55 km range, low-level sea-skimming to evade radar, and a solid booster for launch. This test follows the recent commissioning of INS Arighat, enhancing India's naval deterrence.[3]

## Location
Bay of Bengal, off Odisha, India

## Source Context
Reporting referenced from coverage tracked by the @Minhdr18 OSINT beat. The cited outlet provides additional context, attribution, and verification of the claims summarized above.

## Tags
India, NASM-SR, anti-ship missile, DRDO, naval test, Bay of Bengal, defense tech
